articles.summary: Significant funds have been allocated for the preservation of historical documents and archives. These efforts aim to safeguard valuable historical records and make them accessible for research and public education, enriching national heritage.

articles.headline: British hedge fund Qube Research to establish office in GIFT City
articles.summary: London-based high-frequency trader Qube Research & Technologies (QRT) is setting up operations in GIFT City, making it the first global HFT in India’s International Financial Services Centre. QRT plans to launch a Category III fund to benefit from tax exemptions on derivative trading and securities transaction tax, positioning the SEZ as a hub for sophisticated global trading strategies.

articles.headline: ITC adjusted net profit rises 6% to Rs 5,470 crore in Q4
articles.summary: ITC reported a 6.1% growth in consolidated net profit from continuing operations for Q4FY26, driven by strong performance in cigarettes and FMCG. However, its reported net profit fell 72.7% to Rs 5,387.97 crore due to a high base from a one-time gain of Rs 15,145 crore in the year-ago period related to its hotel business demerger. Gross revenue rose 16.9% to Rs 23,821.48 crore.

articles.headline: Major Indian banks adopt hybrid work models after PM's austerity call
articles.summary: Lenders including HDFC Bank, IndusInd Bank, and Yes Bank have implemented hybrid work-from-home arrangements following Prime Minister Narendra Modi's call for energy-saving and resource conservation. HDFC Bank introduced a trial WFH model for up to two days a week for corporate staff, while others like Axis and Citi continued existing pandemic-era hybrid policies to maintain efficiency.

articles.headline: Coforge launches Aeronova.AI platform for airline retailing
articles.summary: IT services firm Coforge has introduced Aeronova.AI, a platform designed to transform airline retailing using generative AI. The solution aims to help airlines move from digital ambitions to practical execution by enhancing customer experiences and streamlining retail operations in the aviation sector.

articles.headline: Sansera Engineering Q4 net profit more than doubles to Rs 121 crore
articles.summary: Sansera Engineering reported a sharp rise in its financial performance for the March quarter of 2025-26. The company’s consolidated net profit exceeded Rs 121 crore, representing a growth of more than 100% year-on-year. The positive results led to a 14.7% surge in the company's share price following the announcement.

articles.headline: Rategain Travel Technologies reports 28% growth in Q4 profit
articles.summary: Rategain Travel Technologies posted a 28% year-on-year increase in its net profit for Q4FY26, reaching Rs 70 crore. Despite the profit growth, the company's stock price saw a marginal decline of 1.1% in the recent trading session.

articles.headline: Marsons bags $3.1 million solar energy order from US developer
articles.summary: Electrical equipment manufacturer Marsons has secured a contract worth $3.1 million from a solar energy developer based in the United States. The international order reflects the company’s expanding footprint in the renewable energy infrastructure sector.

articles.headline: Automakers propose phased flex-fuel vehicle rollout and GST cuts
articles.summary: Executives from major car and two-wheeler firms have told the Petroleum Ministry that a phased rollout and a GST cut for flex-fuel two-wheelers are the best ways to drive adoption. Flex fuel vehicles, which run on high-ethanol blends, are currently more expensive to build than petrol models and require significant infrastructure investment to be viable for consumers.

articles.headline: Emami Q4 net profit declines 11.7% to Rs 143 crore
articles.summary: FMCG firm Emami Ltd reported an 11.72% decline in consolidated net profit to Rs 143.17 crore for the fourth quarter ended March 31. The company cited unfavorable seasonal conditions affecting its summer portfolio and geopolitical disruptions in West Asia, which caused a 5% decline in its international business.

articles.headline: LG Electronics India Q4 profit dips 8% on margin pressure
articles.summary: LG Electronics India saw its net profit decline 8.2% in Q4FY26 as operating margins were squeezed by rupee depreciation and high commodity prices. Despite the profit dip, net sales rose 8.1% to Rs 8,054 crore, supported by strong demand for premium products such as large-panel TVs, five-star air conditioners, and high-end refrigerators.

articles.headline: JSW Cement Q4 profit rises 11-fold on tax benefits
articles.summary: JSW Cement reported a net profit of Rs 371.33 crore in Q4FY26, a nearly 11-fold increase year-on-year. The surge was primarily driven by a one-time deferred tax benefit of Rs 211.2 crore following the company's move to a new tax regime. Revenue from operations also grew 10.86% to Rs 1,895 crore due to higher sales volumes.

articles.headline: Max Healthcare Q4 net profit rises 7% to Rs 342 crore
articles.summary: Max Healthcare Institute reported a 7.2% year-on-year rise in consolidated net profit for the March quarter. Revenue rose 12% to Rs 2,143 crore, aided by an 8% increase in occupied bed days and a 12% jump in international patient revenue, which now accounts for 9% of total earnings. Margins remained under pressure due to expansion-related costs.

articles.headline: India's outbound FDI declines 10.8% to $5.6 billion in April
articles.summary: India's outward foreign direct investment dropped by 10.8% to $5.6 billion in April 2026, down from $6.33 billion a year earlier. While equity commitments surged to $3.37 billion, the overall total was dragged down by a sharp drop in debt and guarantees for overseas units, according to data released by the Reserve Bank of India.

articles.headline: CJI Surya Kant asks High Courts to conduct online hearings
articles.summary: Chief Justice of India Surya Kant has requested all high courts in the country to implement online hearings. During a Supreme Court session, the CJI noted that most high courts have already complied. He emphasized that the shift to virtual proceedings should be a voluntary exercise involving both the Bar and the Bench in the interest of the nation.

articles.headline: Piyush Goyal considering steps to contain widening current account deficit
articles.summary: Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal stated that the government is considering several measures to manage the CAD as merchandise imports surged to a six-month high. While Goyal ruled out immediate cuts to non-essential imports, he urged large corporations to pay MSME suppliers within 45 days to maintain liquidity. He also highlighted upcoming trade deal negotiations with the United States.

articles.headline: Panic buying caused fuel shortages at some Indian retail outlets
articles.summary: The government reported that fuel shortages at certain retail pumps were primarily due to a 20-30% spike in demand caused by panic buying. Additionally, bulk diesel buyers moved to retail stations due to a price difference of Rs 40-42 per litre. Officials stated that refineries are operating at full capacity and stock levels are being continuously monitored and replenished.

articles.headline: Arvind Panagariya advises RBI not to defend rupee against 100/$
articles.summary: Finance Commission Chairman Arvind Panagariya urged the Reserve Bank of India not to let the psychological mark of Rs 100 per dollar determine policy. He argued that defending the currency would bleed reserves and that the economy is better positioned than in 2013 to absorb inflationary pressure from depreciation. The rupee recently snapped a 9-day losing streak to close at 96.20/$.

articles.headline: West Asia war to weigh on India's growth and inflation
articles.summary: S Mahendra Dev, chairman of the EAC-PM, warned that the West Asia conflict will impact India's fiscal economy and supply chains even if it ends soon. He noted that crude oil prices could influence growth and inflation in FY27, potentially requiring a fresh estimation of targets if prices reach $120 a barrel. He called for a structured risk-management framework and strategic reserves.

articles.headline: US Russian oil waiver terms may crimp India's import gains
articles.summary: A new 30-day US waiver extension for Russian oil imports contains loading date restrictions that may limit volume availability for Indian refiners in June. While imports hit 2.4 million barrels per day this week, the latest terms only authorize cargoes loaded before April 17, 2026. Uncertainty over sanctions has already caused May loading projections to drop significantly.

articles.headline: LIC reports record Rs 57,419 crore annual net profit for FY26
articles.summary: LIC’s net profit for Q4FY26 grew 23.18% to Rs 23,420.43 crore, supported by higher premium and investment income. For the full fiscal year, the state-owned insurer posted a record net profit of Rs 57,419 crore. LIC maintained its leadership in the life insurance sector with a 56.66% market share and recommended a final dividend of Rs 10 per share for the year.

articles.headline: GAIL Q4 net profit drops 38% due to gas supply disruptions
articles.summary: State-owned GAIL (India) Ltd reported a 38% fall in March quarter profit to Rs 1,262.18 crore. The performance was severely impacted by gas supply disruptions linked to the war in West Asia, which affected imports from Qatar. The utility booked losses in its natural gas marketing and petrochemical segments, leading to an overall profit plunge to Rs 6,968 crore for the full fiscal year.

articles.headline: Sri Lanka imposes 50% surcharge on imported motor vehicles
articles.summary: Sri Lanka has introduced a temporary 50% surcharge on imported vehicles, including buses and passenger cars, for three months. The move aims to protect foreign exchange reserves following a massive 1,200% year-on-year surge in vehicle imports in early 2026. India remains a key supplier, with cars making up 5.46% of total Indian exports to Sri Lanka in the last fiscal year.

articles.headline: India's currency in circulation hits record high of Rs 42.86 trillion
articles.summary: Currency in circulation (CiC) rose 11.5% year-on-year to a record Rs 42.86 trillion as of May 15. The increase was driven by election-related spending, stronger rural demand, and precautionary cash holdings by households amid financial asset volatility. CiC expanded by Rs 1.15 trillion in the first six weeks of the current fiscal year despite the growth of digital payments.

articles.headline: RBI board to decide on FY26 surplus transfer to government
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India’s central board is scheduled to meet on Friday to finalize the surplus transfer to the government for FY26. Economists expect the payout to range between Rs 2.8 trillion and Rs 3.3 trillion, potentially exceeding last year’s record dividend of Rs 2.1 trillion. The transfer will be supported by higher interest income and a potential reduction in contingency buffers.

articles.headline: Uttar Pradesh to invest Rs 600 crore to become AI hub
articles.summary: To position Uttar Pradesh as a leading AI hub in South Asia, the state government will invest nearly Rs 600 crore. The plan includes Rs 230 crore for the UP AI Mission and Rs 370 crore to develop India’s first 'AI City' in Lucknow. The 260-acre innovation zone will focus on hardware, compute infrastructure, and deep-tech solutions to accelerate research and commercialization.

articles.headline: Bar Council of India seeks SC approval for women's representation
articles.summary: The Bar Council of India has moved the Supreme Court to approve a merit-based 'co-option' mechanism to ensure 30% women's representation in state bar councils. The proposal seeks to fill a 10% quota by selecting female runners-up from elections, complementing the existing 20% reserved seats. The BCI aims to ensure women have a rightful place in the legal fraternity’s representative structure.

articles.headline: Rajasthan installs over 200,000 rooftop solar plants under PMSGY
articles.summary: More than 200,000 rooftop solar power plants have been installed across Rajasthan under the Prime Minister Surya Ghar Muft Bijli Yojana. The state government’s scheme, which offers 150 units of free electricity and significant subsidies, has resulted in a total capacity of 771 MW. The initiative aims to provide energy security and connect citizens with pollution-free energy.

articles.headline: SAIL plans Rs 35,000 crore capital expenditure over two years
articles.summary: State-run Steel Authority of India Ltd (SAIL) has lined up a capex plan of over Rs 35,000 crore for the next two financial years. The investment will focus on capacity expansion at the IISCO, Bokaro, and Bhilai steel plants. FY27 will see an initial spend of Rs 15,000 crore, increasing to over Rs 20,000 crore in FY28 as major expansion projects progress.

articles.headline: Employment in India's unincorporated sector crosses 150 million mark
articles.summary: Employment in India’s unincorporated non-agricultural sector rose 15.51% year-on-year to reach 151.7 million in the January-March quarter. National Statistics Office data shows a healthy labor market expansion, with the services segment acting as the primary driver. The number of establishments also rose by 16.7% to 91.6 million, with growth being particularly strong in rural areas.

articles.headline: PM Modi's five-nation tour secures $40 billion in investment commitments
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s visits to the UAE, Netherlands, Sweden, Norway, and Italy resulted in fresh investment commitments totaling nearly $40 billion. The commitments from over 50 global CEOs cover sectors including semiconductors, green infrastructure, and logistics. The tour also strengthened energy security through LPG supply and strategic petroleum reserve pacts with Abu Dhabi.

articles.headline: Karnataka startups valuation crosses $300 billion milestone
articles.summary: Startups based in Karnataka have reached a total valuation exceeding $300 billion, led by a boom in deep-tech sectors. IT Minister Priyank Kharge stated that the state accounts for the highest number of funded deep-tech startups in India, particularly in semiconductors and space tech. The government is focusing on building infrastructure outside Bengaluru to support this ecosystem.

articles.headline: US authorities shut down Indian call centre for defrauding elderly
articles.summary: American authorities have closed an India-based call centre operation that allegedly defrauded hundreds of elderly Americans of millions of dollars through tech support scams. Following a multi-year FBI investigation, five fraudsters and two senior executives were convicted. The scam involved routing fraudulent calls to victims in the US and abroad.

articles.headline: Nibe successfully completes first technical trials of Vayu Astra-1
articles.summary: Pune-based defense manufacturer Nibe has successfully completed technical trials for its 100-km-range loitering munition system, 'Vayu Astra-1'. During tests at Pokhran and Joshimath, the system struck a target 100 km away with precision. The development marks a milestone for the 'Make in India' initiative in precision-guided weapon systems. The company's stock rose nearly 30% over the week.

articles.headline: SBI staff federation threatens two-day nationwide strike from May 25
articles.summary: The All India State Bank of India Staff Federation has threatened a nationwide strike to demand recruitment of adequate personnel and an end to outsourcing permanent jobs. If the strike occurs, SBI operations could be disrupted for five days, as it falls before a weekend and a scheduled public holiday for Eid al-Adha on May 27.

articles.headline: Cyprus President highlights infinite trade opportunities with India and EU
articles.summary: Visiting Cyprus President Nikos Christodoulides described India and the European Union as natural partners during an event at the NSE. Pitching Cyprus as a gateway for Indian companies to the EU market, he emphasized that combining two of the world's largest democracies offers infinite economic opportunities for both regions.

articles.headline: Automakers seek 5% GST for range-extended electric vehicles
articles.summary: Leading global and local automakers are pushing the government to lower the GST on range-extended electric vehicles (REEVs) to 5%, matching battery electric vehicles. Unlike plug-in hybrids, REEVs use a combustion engine solely as a generator to charge batteries. Proponents argue REEVs help eliminate range anxiety, while pure EV manufacturers like Tata and Mahindra oppose the move.

articles.headline: Apollo HealthCo eyes cash breakeven by Q1 FY27
articles.summary: Apollo HealthCo, the digital health and pharmacy subsidiary of Apollo Hospitals, expects to achieve cash breakeven in the current quarter. The entity is undergoing a major restructuring including a demerger from its parent and a merger with its pharmacy distribution arm Keimed. The process is expected to be completed by the fourth quarter of FY27.

articles.headline: Essel Group celebrates 100 years with future focus on technology
articles.summary: Founded in 1926, the Essel Group marked its centenary by announcing a future strategy centered on technology. The conglomerate, which launched India's first private satellite channel Zee TV, plans to build future-ready platforms to capture the 'attention economy.' The group has generated employment for over 10 million people across media, real estate, and infrastructure sectors.

articles.headline: Nvidia profit surges 211% to $58.3 billion amid AI boom
articles.summary: Nvidia reported a quarterly profit of $58.3 billion, a 211% increase from the previous year, as the 'AI factory' build-out accelerates. While data centers remain its main revenue source, CEO Jensen Huang highlighted progress in diversifying into robots and automated vehicles. Despite beating analyst estimates, shares remained steady in premarket trading as investors weighed new competition.

articles.headline: German Chancellor proposes associate EU membership for Ukraine
articles.summary: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz has proposed a new 'associate member' status for Ukraine to allow its participation in EU summits and ministerial meetings without voting rights. Merz suggested the move could facilitate a peace deal to end the war with Russia by creating substantial security guarantees and a clear, interim route toward full EU membership.

articles.headline: US indicts former Cuban President Raul Castro on murder charges
articles.summary: Former Cuban President Raul Castro has been indicted in the US on murder charges related to a 1996 incident where Cuban jets shot down planes operated by exiles. The indictment of the 94-year-old leader marks a significant escalation in Washington's pressure campaign against the island's communist government. Castro was last seen in public in Cuba earlier this month.

articles.headline: Samsung chip workers to receive average $340,000 bonus
articles.summary: Samsung Electronics plans to distribute approximately $26.6 billion in bonuses to its 78,000 semiconductor division employees after averting a labor strike. Following booming profits in memory chips, workers will receive an average of $340,000 each. The payout reflects the massive surge in operating profit driven by the global artificial intelligence boom.

articles.headline: China's President Xi Jinping likely to visit North Korea
articles.summary: Chinese President Xi Jinping may visit North Korea as early as next week to act as a mediator between Kim Jong Un and US President Donald Trump. Reports indicate that Chinese security and protocol officials were recently in Pyongyang to prepare for the visit. Xi hosted Trump last week and is reportedly seeking to stabilize nuclear program negotiations in the region.

articles.headline: Eli Lilly obesity drug retatrutide shows 28% weight loss
articles.summary: Eli Lilly reported that its experimental obesity drug retatrutide helped patients lose more than 28% of their body weight over a year and a half in key clinical trials. The results position retatrutide as potentially the most powerful drug in the weight-loss market. The company plans to seek regulatory approval next year, with latest data easing concerns about nerve-related side effects.

articles.headline: New Ebola case confirmed in eastern Democratic Republic of Congo
articles.summary: Health authorities have confirmed an Ebola case in South Kivu province, hundreds of kilometers from the current outbreak’s epicenter in Ituri. The spread signals a growing emergency that has already been linked to 139 deaths and 600 suspected cases. The World Health Organization currently considers the global risk low but has declared it a public health emergency of concern in the region.

articles.headline: Walmart shares fall 7% despite meeting sales growth targets
articles.summary: Walmart stuck to its conservative annual sales and profit targets, causing its shares to decline 7% despite reporting a 7.1% rise in quarterly sales to $175.7 billion. The retailer cited pressure on consumer spending and rising fuel costs as primary concerns. Walmart remains focused on its scale to keep prices low for bargain-hunting shoppers amid sticky inflation.

articles.headline: SpaceX files for record IPO despite $4.3 billion quarterly loss
articles.summary: Elon Musk's SpaceX has publicly filed for an initial public offering that could be the largest in history, targeting a $2 trillion valuation. The filing revealed a net loss of $4.28 billion on revenue of $4.69 billion for the first quarter of 2026. Musk will retain 85.1% voting control through super-voting Class B shares. Marketing for the share sale is expected to begin in June.

articles.headline: Stephen Colbert to end Late Show run after two decades
articles.summary: The CBS 'Late Show' with Stephen Colbert will air its final episode on May 21, marking the end of a two-decade era in political comedy. Colbert transitioned from his satirical character on 'The Colbert Report' to mainstream success as a late-night host. His exit comes amid significant shifts in the television landscape and the decline of traditional late-night monoculture.

articles.headline: Proposed draft Bill triggers audit cooling-off concerns for Indian firms
articles.summary: A draft Bill introduced in Parliament proposes a mandatory three-year 'cooling-off' period for auditors, prohibiting them from providing non-audit services to former clients. While aimed at eliminating conflicts of interest, the move has raised concerns among mid-sized audit firms and MSMEs. Industry experts warn the provision could lead to market concentration among the 'Big Four'.

articles.headline: Amitabh Kant calls for structural reforms in India's fertiliser sector
articles.summary: Former NITI Aayog CEO Amitabh Kant has urged structural reforms in the fertiliser sector to counter global supply shocks. Writing in an op-ed, he proposed moving urea under the Nutrient-Based Subsidy framework and shifting toward direct benefit transfers to farmers. Kant also highlighted green ammonia as a domestically producible alternative to reduce import reliance and carbon emissions.

articles.headline: CCI rules Delhi hospitals are not automatic monopolies over patients
articles.summary: The Competition Commission of India (CCI) ruled that super-specialty hospitals do not automatically become separate monopoly 'aftermarkets' for admitted patients. While acknowledging significant practical lock-in effects regarding in-house pharmacies and labs, the commission declined to fully endorse findings of abuse of dominance against major Delhi hospitals in 12 separate orders.

articles.headline: Radisson to launch India's first stadium hotel in Nathdwara
articles.summary: Radisson Hotel Group is opening India’s first stadium hotel in Nathdwara, Rajasthan, where guests can watch cricket matches from their rooms. The 234-room hotel is part of Radisson’s push to reach 500 hotels in India by 2030. The chain is also in talks with the Himachal Pradesh Cricket Association for a second similar project at the Dharamshala stadium.

articles.headline: Skymet to challenge NCDEX rainfall futures launch in court
articles.summary: Private forecaster Skymet Weather Services announced it will legally challenge NCDEX's launch of weather derivatives in partnership with the IMD. Skymet claims the exchange used its baseline data and campaign know-how for three years before sidelining it. The NCDEX RAIN-MUMBAI contract, scheduled for May 29, is designed to help farmers hedge against climate risks like erratic rainfall.

articles.headline: Grasim Industries beats Q4 estimates driven by VSF and chemicals
articles.summary: Grasim Industries outperformed analyst estimates in Q4FY26 as EBITDA rose 2.5 times year-on-year to Rs 540 crore. Performance was bolstered by margin expansion in Viscose Staple Fibre and strong volume growth in chemicals. The company’s new paint brand, Birla Opus, implement price hikes to offset input costs, with analysts remaining bullish on its long-term growth prospects.

articles.headline: Long-duration and gilt mutual funds lose shine as yields spike
articles.summary: Rising government security (G-Sec) yields amid the West Asia crisis have eroded near-term gains for long-duration and gilt mutual fund schemes. Average one-year returns for gilt funds fell to -1%, underperforming lower-risk short-duration funds. Experts advise caution as bond yields hit two-year highs, making higher-risk debt funds vulnerable to continued global uncertainty.

articles.headline: Rupee snaps nine-day losing streak to become top Asian performer
articles.summary: The Indian rupee appreciated 65 paise to settle at 96.20 per dollar on Thursday, ending a record losing streak. The recovery was aided by aggressive RBI intervention in the non-deliverable forwards market and easing crude oil prices. Despite the recovery, some market analysts expect a potential interest rate hike in June to counter inflationary risks from high energy prices.

articles.headline: Reliance slows Jio Platforms IPO preparations amid West Asia tensions
articles.summary: Reliance Industries has reportedly slowed preparations for its potential $4 billion Jio Platforms IPO, India’s largest-ever share sale. The delay comes as the conglomerate reviews the deal's structure in response to geopolitical tensions in Iran and recent market volatility. While draft paperwork is still planned, key stakeholders have reportedly slowed decision-making processes.

articles.headline: Analysts remain cautious on Ola Electric despite Q4 improvements
articles.summary: Ola Electric shares fell 3.9% as analysts expressed doubts about the sustainability of its Q4 recovery. Although gross margins improved to 38.5% due to production-linked incentives, vehicle deliveries fell 61% year-on-year. Brokerages cited concerns over volume growth durability, cash conservation, and intensifying competition from established players like Ather and TVS.

articles.headline: Lai Ching-te happy to speak with Trump, says ministry
articles.summary: Taiwan's foreign ministry stated President Lai Ching-te would be open to speaking with Donald Trump. The ministry noted that Taiwanese presidents have not spoken directly with US presidents since Washington shifted diplomatic recognition to Beijing from Taipei in 1979. This follows reports of potential shifts in US-Taiwan relations.

articles.headline: Australia fines Elon Musk's X for child safety reporting failure
articles.summary: An Australian judge has fined Elon Musk’s social media platform X 610,000 Australian dollars ($465,000) for failing to provide information to an online safety watchdog. The regulator had requested details on how the platform tackled child sexual exploitation content. X's appeal against the fine was rejected by the court.

articles.headline: Supreme Court: Even PMLA accused has right to be heard
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that an accused person under the Prevention of Money Laundering Act (PMLA) has the right to be heard before a court takes cognizance of an offense. Justices M Sundresh and S V N Bhatti stated that provisions of the CrPC or BNSS, which ensure the right of an accused to a fair trial, cannot be dispensed with even in PMLA cases.

articles.headline: PM Modi signals fresh deregulation push at high-level meeting
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi chaired a meeting with the council of ministers and NITI Aayog members to push for further deregulation of the economy. The discussions focused on removing unnecessary regulatory hurdles that affect citizens' lives and business operations. The PM urged ministers to focus on reforms that simplify compliance and improve ease of living.

articles.headline: Two techies arrested for running fake US tax advice scam
articles.summary: Karnataka police arrested two software engineers in Bengaluru for allegedly defrauding US citizens of ₹40 crore. Operating from call centres in Koramangala and HSR Layout, the duo posed as tax consultants for Intuit QuickBooks to trick users into purchasing unnecessary software or services. They reportedly used fake American identities and routed money via shell companies.

articles.headline: N Chandrasekaran to present roadmap for loss-making Tata businesses
articles.summary: Tata Sons Chairman N Chandrasekaran is set to present a detailed roadmap for new businesses and loss-making units at a crucial board meeting on May 26. The presentation will cover entities including BigBasket, Tata 1mg, Croma, and Tata Neu, which reported a combined loss of ₹5,700 crore in FY24. Air India’s performance and future strategy will also be discussed.

articles.headline: Tamil Nadu cabinet expanded with seven Dalit ministers
articles.summary: Chief Minister M K Stalin expanded his cabinet by inducting seven Dalit ministers, the highest representation for the community in the state's history. The cabinet now has 33 ministers. The expansion includes V Ganesan, C V Ganesan, N Kayalvizhi Selvi, and others. The move is seen as a strategic push for social engineering and Dalit representation in the DMK-led government.

articles.headline: Corruption overflowing in every government department: Karnataka Upalokayukta
articles.summary: Karnataka Upalokayukta Justice B Veerappa stated that corruption is rampant across all government departments. Speaking at a grievance redressal meet in Mandya, he noted that 1,281 cases of corruption were registered across 16 districts. He urged officials to discharge their duties with honesty and warned that serious action would be taken against those found guilty of graft.

articles.headline: NEET-UG 2026: CBI grills doctor and teacher in paper leak
articles.summary: The CBI questioned a junior college teacher, a beauty parlour owner, and a doctor in Pune as part of the NEET-UG 2026 paper leak investigation. The agency is tracking money trails and digital footprints of beneficiaries who allegedly received the leaked papers. The probe has expanded to several states following the arrest of several intermediaries and local kingpins.

articles.headline: Karnataka CET counselling to proceed without NEET results
articles.summary: The Karnataka Examinations Authority (KEA) will proceed with the Common Entrance Test (CET) seat allotment process for engineering and other courses in June, without waiting for the delayed NEET results. The decision aims to avoid further disruption to the academic calendar. Seats reserved for medical students in CET rounds will be filled later once NEET ranks are declared.

articles.headline: Landless individuals seek IT exemption for agricultural income
articles.summary: The Central Board of Direct Taxes (CBDT) has identified over 300 cases where individuals without any agricultural land claimed income tax exemptions under the guise of agricultural income. Total exemptions claimed in these cases amount to ₹2,038 crore. Investigations revealed that non-genuine products were sold as farm produce to claim tax benefits.

articles.headline: Record 274 climbers summit Mount Everest in a single day
articles.summary: Mount Everest saw its busiest day ever as 274 people reached the summit within 24 hours during a brief weather window. Among them were three Indians from Hyderabad. The previous single-day record was 223 climbers in May 2019. Despite the massive rush, no major fatalities were reported during the record-breaking window.

articles.headline: Bengaluru cybercrime: Don't click on fake wedding invitations
articles.summary: Cybercriminals are using fake wedding invitation APK files on WhatsApp to gain remote access to victims' phones. Once installed, the malware allows hackers to steal contacts, read SMS, and drain bank accounts through UPI. Over 10 such cases were reported in Bengaluru recently, with victims losing amounts ranging from ₹55,000 to over ₹5 lakh.

articles.headline: II PU Exam-2: Freshers' pass percentage at 46.22%
articles.summary: The Karnataka II PU Exam-2 results showed a pass percentage of 46.22% for freshers. The overall pass percentage for the supplementary and improvement exam stood at 35.25%. Girls outperformed boys with a 38.01% pass rate compared to 33.15% for boys. Science students had the highest pass percentage at 43.15%. Total 1.5 lakh students appeared for the exam.

articles.headline: Fierce rain and hailstorm batter Bengaluru, Metro services hit
articles.summary: Bengaluru was hit by heavy rain, hailstorms, and winds reaching 46kmph, leading to waterlogging and traffic snarls. Metro services on the Purple and Green lines were briefly disrupted due to technical issues caused by the storm. Over 50 trees were uprooted across the city. The IMD has issued an orange alert for more rain and thunderstorms over the next 48 hours.

articles.headline: Ejipura flyover completion deadline extended to October 2026
articles.summary: The long-delayed Ejipura flyover in Bengaluru has been given a new completion deadline of October 2026. BBMP officials stated that 70% of the work on the 2.6km flyover is complete. The contractor has been directed to ramp up construction speed. The project has faced multiple delays and legal hurdles since it began in 2017.

articles.headline: Medical experts propose renaming PCOS to PMOS
articles.summary: Health experts at the European Congress of Endocrinology have proposed renaming Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS) to Polyendocrine Metabolic Ovarian Syndrome (PMOS). The change aims to better reflect the condition's complex endocrine and metabolic nature rather than just focusing on ovarian cysts. PMOS affects 10-15% of women in Bengaluru, often leading to insulin resistance.

articles.headline: Holiday club ordered to refund ₹91,000 to Bengaluru man
articles.summary: The District Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission ordered a holiday club to refund ₹91,419 to a Bengaluru resident. The consumer had been pressured into signing a one-sided membership agreement and was denied a refund when he tried to cancel within three months. The commission ruled the contract was unfair and lacked transparency regarding cancellation terms.

articles.headline: Express bus service to Hoskote from Nelamangala soon
articles.summary: The BMTC is set to launch express bus services on the 85.6km Satellite Town Ring Road (STRR) stretch, connecting Nelamangala to Hoskote in 2.4 hours. The service aims to provide faster transit for commuters by bypassing city traffic. Total 11 non-AC buses will operate on this route with 28 scheduled trips daily. The one-way fare is fixed at ₹110.

articles.headline: Bengaluru swimming coach leads team across Strait of Gibraltar
articles.summary: Bengaluru swimming coach Srikanth Viswanathan led a four-member team to successfully swim across the 14km Strait of Gibraltar in 4 hours and 33 minutes. The team, which included swimmers from Mumbai and Nashik, battled strong currents and cold waters. This achievement is part of the 'Ocean's Seven' challenge, with Viswanathan completing his second channel swim.

articles.headline: BMRDA allowed to sell civic amenity sites with conditions
articles.summary: The Bengaluru Metropolitan Region Development Authority (BMRDA) has been authorized to sell civic amenity (CA) sites that have remained unutilized for 10 years. New rules allow for the sale or lease of these sites for social infrastructure like schools and hospitals. Buyers must pay a one-time lump sum of 30% of the site value and are prohibited from further sub-dividing the land.

articles.headline: RTE quota for SC students extended to Class 10
articles.summary: The Karnataka government has extended the Right to Education (RTE) quota for SC/ST students up to Class 10. Previously, the quota was only till Class 8. The move will benefit students who would have had to leave their current schools after Class 8. Schools have been instructed to allow these students to continue their education without charging fees.

articles.headline: Gig workers seek better pay amid rising fuel costs
articles.summary: Gig workers in Bengaluru, including delivery partners and bike-taxi riders, are demanding a rollback of fuel price hikes and better payouts. The United Food Delivery Partners' Union stated that rising petrol prices have significantly reduced workers' daily earnings. Some workers reported spending over 10 hours on the road to earn a net income of less than ₹500 per day.

articles.headline: Multivitamin demand spikes in India due to generic semaglutide
articles.summary: The entry of generic semaglutide in the Indian market has led to an 11.6% spike in multivitamin sales. Doctors are increasingly prescribing vitamin supplements to patients on weight-loss and diabetes medication to counter potential nutritional deficiencies. The overall multivitamin market in India has reached ₹780 crore in the last quarter.

articles.headline: IN-SPACE to transfer PSLV technology to private sector
articles.summary: The Indian National Space Promotion and Authorisation Centre (IN-SPACE) is set to transfer the technology for the Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle (PSLV) to a private consortium. The ₹860-crore deal with HAL and L&T will allow private firms to manufacture and launch PSLVs. This marks a significant step in the commercialization of India’s space industry.

articles.headline: Indian Navy to receive 45 new warships in 3-4 years
articles.summary: The Indian Navy is set to receive 45 new warships and vessels within the next three to four years as part of its modernization plan. Vice Admiral Vatsyan revealed that the Navy has an in-principle approval for 195 vessels in total. The target is to reach a 160-ship fleet by 2030 and 200 ships by 2035 to counter increasing maritime challenges in the Indo-Pacific.

articles.headline: NTA to ramp up in-house capabilities for national exams
articles.summary: The National Testing Agency (NTA) announced plans to strengthen its in-house capabilities to conduct exams like NEET and JEE. The move follows recent concerns over paper leaks and malpractice in exams conducted by third-party agencies. The NTA will transition to computer-based testing for more exams and enhance its secure logistics and digital infrastructure.

articles.headline: India weighing steps to check gold imports and CAD
articles.summary: The Union Commerce Ministry is considering measures to curb rising gold imports and manage the widening Current Account Deficit (CAD). Officials are monitoring the situation as gold imports reached record levels recently. Potential steps include raising import duties or introducing gold deposit schemes to encourage domestic recycling over fresh imports.

articles.headline: Nvidia hits record quarterly revenue of $81.6 billion
articles.summary: US chip giant Nvidia reported record quarterly revenue of $81.6 billion, driven by massive demand for its AI-powered hardware. The company's net profit surged to $58.3 billion, far exceeding market expectations. Nvidia also announced an $80 billion share buyback program, solidifying its position as the world's most valuable semiconductor company.

articles.headline: Dope traffickers to face 10-year jail and ₹5 lakh fine
articles.summary: The Indian government has proposed amendments to the National Anti-Doping Act to include strict criminal penalties for drug traffickers. Under the new rules, individuals involved in supplying or distributing banned substances to athletes could face up to 10 years in prison and a fine of ₹5 lakh. The move aims to curb the use of performance-enhancing drugs in sports.

articles.headline: India's Peak Power Demand Hits Record 270.82 GW Amid Heatwave
articles.summary: India's peak power demand hit a record 270.82 GW on Thursday, marking the fourth consecutive daily record, driven by severe heatwaves across Northern and Western India. This surpassed Wednesday's 265.44 GW and Tuesday's 237 GW. The Ministry of Power expects demand to cross 250 GW, possibly 283 GW in June, and 275 GW due to El Nino. While surging solar generation (22%) helps, localized outages emerge. Thermal power contributes 62.8%, with coal supply ensured.

articles.headline: Trump, Netanyahu Hold Tense Call Over Iran War, Diplomacy, and Strikes
articles.summary: US President Trump and Israeli PM Netanyahu held a tense call over the Iran war and a proposed peace pact. Netanyahu expressed skepticism over Iran's nuclear compliance and sought renewed strikes on critical infrastructure. Trump, favoring diplomacy, defended the process and revealed he delayed strikes after requests from Qatar and UAE, but warned Iran faces fresh attacks if no flexibility is shown. Israel remains on high alert for regional attacks.

articles.headline: Calcutta High Court Orders CBI SIT Probe into RG Kar Doctor Murder
articles.summary: The Calcutta High Court ordered a three-member CBI Special Investigation Team to probe the 2024 rape-and-murder case of a trainee doctor at RG Kar Hospital. The SIT will investigate allegations by the victim's parents of a larger conspiracy and evidence tampering, focusing on the timeline from her last meal to cremation. A report is due by June 25. The court also rejected TMC MP Abhishek Banerjee's plea but granted interim protection.

articles.headline: Bengal to Hand Detained Bangladeshi Infiltrators Directly to BSF
articles.summary: West Bengal CM Suvendu Adhikari announced that illegal Bangladeshi infiltrators detained by state police or RPF will be handed directly to the BSF for deportation, bypassing courts. This "detect, delete and deport" framework, following the Immigration and Foreigners Act, 2025, aims to streamline the removal of individuals not eligible for citizenship under the CAA.

articles.headline: UK Net Migration Halves; Indian Students, Workers Lead Exit
articles.summary: Britain's net migration nearly halved to 171,000 in 2025, down from 944,000, due to a tightening immigration regime. Indians led the exit, with 51,000 students and 21,000 workers leaving, the highest among all nationalities, though they still lead in Health and Care Worker extensions. The UK Home Secretary attributed the decline to new skills-based systems and efforts to end reliance on cheap overseas labor.

articles.headline: X Withholds Satirical 'Cockroach Janta Party' Account Amid Rising Popularity
articles.summary: The satirical 'Cockroach Janta Party' (CJP) gained over 18 million Instagram followers, surpassing major political parties, by critiquing political issues, unemployment, and economic anxiety with humor. Following controversial remarks by CJI Surya Kant on unemployed youth, X (formerly Twitter) withheld CJP's account in India after a legal demand, a move the founder expected as the movement resonated widely with youth.

articles.headline: Murder Convict-Turned-Bollywood Actor Arrested After 12 Years On Run
articles.summary: Murder convict Hemant Nagindas Modi, 53, was arrested in Ahmedabad after 12 years on the run. Convicted for a 2005 murder, he jumped parole in 2014. During his evasion, he lived in Mumbai under false identities, working as a supporting actor in Bollywood films like 'Thugs of Hindostan' and 'Jai Mummy Di', and TV shows like 'Wagle Ki Duniya'.

articles.headline: US Secretary Rubio, Venezuela President to Visit India for Energy Talks
articles.summary: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio will visit India next week for talks on energy and technology cooperation. Venezuela’s interim President Delcy Rodriguez is also traveling to New Delhi to discuss energy sales, as India seeks fuel alternatives amid the West Asia conflict. Rubio, who noted US interest in selling energy to India, is expected to hold talks regarding Rodriguez, underscoring deepening Indo-US strategic ties.

articles.headline: PM Modi Chairs Council Meeting on Economy, West Asia Crisis, Viksit Bharat
articles.summary: PM Narendra Modi held an extensive 4.5-hour council of ministers meeting in New Delhi, hours after a five-nation foreign tour. He emphasized "Viksit Bharat 2047" requires speed, transparency, and red tape elimination, reviewing nine sectors. Discussions focused on the West Asia conflict's impact on India's economy and global supply chains, stressing the need for fuel alternatives like biogas and a sharper feedback mechanism.

articles.headline: Two Air India Flights Face Incidents: Tail Strike, Emergency Landing
articles.summary: Two Air India flights experienced safety incidents. An A321 from Delhi to Bengaluru (181 passengers) suffered a tail strike during landing at Kempegowda International Airport due to wake turbulence, leading to its grounding and an investigation. Separately, an Air India flight from Bengaluru to Delhi made an emergency landing at Delhi airport after a cockpit fire warning from an engine. All passengers and crew in both incidents disembarked safely.

articles.headline: India-Africa Summit Postponed Due to Ebola Outbreak in Africa
articles.summary: The Fourth India-Africa Forum Summit, initially set for May 28-31 in New Delhi, and the first International Big Cat Alliance meeting, have been postponed due to an evolving Ebola outbreak in parts of Africa, including the Democratic Republic of Congo. This decision, following consultations with the African Union, aims to ensure full participation from over 30 expected heads of state. India has also implemented airport screenings for affected regions.

articles.headline: East Bengal Wins Maiden ISL Title, Ending 22-Year National Drought
articles.summary: East Bengal FC won their maiden Indian Super League title, defeating Inter Kashi 2-1 in a second-half comeback in Kolkata. Goals by Youssef Ezzejjari and Mohammed Rashid/Nandhakumar Sekar secured the victory after Alfred Planas's early lead for Kashi. This triumph ends the club's 22-year drought for a top-tier national title, with East Bengal finishing on 26 points, ahead of Mohun Bagan on superior goal difference.

articles.headline: Karnataka May Move SC Over Voter List Revision Concerns
articles.summary: The Karnataka government is considering approaching the Supreme Court over concerns regarding the Special Intensive Revision (SIR) of electoral rolls. Minister HK Patil noted that preliminary mapping shows voter deletion projections of 8% to 66% in some districts, alleging the process lacks transparency and could lead to large-scale disenfranchisement, citing similar issues in West Bengal where 2.7 million reportedly lost their franchise.

articles.headline: Congress Joins TN Cabinet; Vijay Inducts 23 Ministers in First Coalition
articles.summary: Tamil Nadu CM C. Joseph Vijay inducted 23 new ministers, expanding his cabinet to 33, marking the state's first coalition government. This includes two Congress nominees, returning the party to the state cabinet after 59 years. New portfolios for AI and Digital Services were created, with 11 ministers under 40, seven from Scheduled Caste, and four women. The expansion occurred amidst controversy over the Tamil anthem's placement.

articles.headline: Rajnath Singh Inaugurates Indian War Memorial in South Korea
articles.summary: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh, alongside South Korean officials, inaugurated an Indian War Memorial at Imjingak Park in Seoul. The memorial honors the 60 Para Field Ambulance and the Custodian Force of India for their humanitarian service and medical care during the Korean War, marking its 75th anniversary. Singh emphasized India's enduring contribution to humanitarian assistance on the peninsula.

articles.headline: Pulwama Attack Mastermind Hamza Burhan Killed in PoK
articles.summary: Hamza Burhan, also known as Arjumand Gulzar, a designated terrorist and key mastermind of the 2019 Pulwama attack that killed 40 CRPF personnel, was shot dead by unidentified gunmen in Muzaffarabad, PoK. The Al-Badr commander, operating under heavy security, was responsible for radicalizing youth and involved in recruitment. His death, adding to a list of similar eliminations, is a significant blow to terror networks.

articles.headline: ITC Q4 Revenue Up 17% on Cigarette Sales, Warns on Taxes
articles.summary: ITC Ltd reported a 17% rise in March-quarter revenue to ₹23,821 crore, driven by a 30% surge in cigarette sales, with profit from continuing operations up 6% to ₹5,469.74 crore. FMCG-Others segment profits grew over 1.5 times. The company warned that higher tobacco taxes could boost illicit trade, and geopolitical risks from the West Asia conflict and supply chain disruptions could increase costs for its agri-business.

articles.headline: Quick-Commerce Startup Zepto Plans ₹11,000 Crore IPO in July
articles.summary: Quick commerce platform Zepto, founded by Aadit Palicha and Kaivalya Vohra, is planning an ₹11,000-crore IPO in July, having received SEBI approval. The Bengaluru-based startup, known for its high dark-store density in metro cities, aims to expand its network across tier-1 and tier-2 cities and enhance delivery infrastructure. This is anticipated to be one of India's largest tech IPOs this year, setting it apart from competitors.

articles.headline: Maruti Suzuki to Hike Vehicle Prices by Up to ₹30,000 From June
articles.summary: India's largest carmaker, Maruti Suzuki, announced a price hike of up to ₹30,000 across its entire model range, effective June 2026. The company cited elevated inflationary pressures, adverse commodity costs, rising steel prices, and high logistics costs as reasons for passing on input costs. This move follows similar adjustments by rivals like Hyundai, Tata Motors, and MG Motor India, with other manufacturers also expected to follow suit.

articles.headline: Gujarat Titans Knock Out CSK from IPL Playoffs with 89-Run Win
articles.summary: Gujarat Titans defeated Chennai Super Kings by 89 runs at Narendra Modi Stadium, ending CSK's playoff hopes for the third consecutive season and securing a top-two finish. GT posted 229/4, powered by a record-matching opening stand from Sai Sudharsan (84) and Shubman Gill (64). CSK was bowled out for 140 in 13.4 overs, with Rashid Khan, Kagiso Rabada, and Mohammed Siraj each taking three wickets for GT.

articles.headline: Sports Ministry Proposes Criminal Charges for Doping Trafficking
articles.summary: The Union Sports Ministry proposed amendments to the National Anti-Doping Act, aiming to criminalize the trafficking, illegal supply, and administration of performance-enhancing drugs. Proposed penalties include up to five years in prison and a ₹2 lakh fine, with offenses involving minors or organized crime facing up to 10 years. Athletes will be protected from prosecution, as the laws specifically target criminal networks and support personnel.

articles.headline: Travel Fintech Scapia Raises $63 Million in Series C Funding
articles.summary: Bengaluru-based travel fintech Scapia has secured $63 million in Series C funding, led by General Catalyst with participation from Peak XV Partners and Z47. The startup, which offers co-branded credit cards, a travel marketplace, and AI-curated activities, plans to use the capital to scale its customer base, expand banking partnerships, and enhance its technical platform, capitalizing on increased travel spending by Indian Gen Z and millennials.

articles.headline: Uber, JSW Group Partner to Develop EVs for Indian Ride-Hailing
articles.summary: Ride-hailing giant Uber and JSW Group signed an agreement to jointly develop and deploy electric vehicles tailored specifically for the Indian ride-hailing market. The partnership aims to accelerate EV adoption on Uber's platform with purpose-built solutions for local needs, supporting the government's sustainable urban mobility vision. This follows JSW MG Motor's plan to invest $440 million in Indian expansion.

articles.headline: Amazon India to Launch 100 Global Beauty Brands, Targets Tier-2/3 Cities
articles.summary: Amazon India is expanding its premium beauty portfolio by adding over 100 international and luxury brands like Laura Mercier and Dolce & Gabbana this year. With premium beauty growing 50% YoY and over 50% of demand from Tier-II/III cities, Amazon is making smaller towns central to its strategy. It is also utilizing generative AI and virtual try-on tools, competing for India's projected $40 billion beauty market with Nykaa and Reliance-backed Tira.

articles.headline: Reliance Challenges 'Unjust Enrichment' Claim in SC Gas Dispute
articles.summary: Reliance Industries argued in the Supreme Court that the government's 'unjust enrichment' accusations in a gas migration dispute are misguided. RIL contended its KG-D6 development outperformed state projects, and timely extraction prevented massive losses. The company claimed the government failed to facilitate joint development proposed in 2002 and that accurate recovery predictions were impossible due to subterranean water movements, denying alleged siphoning from ONGC's KG basin deposits.

articles.headline: SpiceJet Faces Legal Action Over $8 Million Unpaid Lease Dues
articles.summary: Aircraft engine lessor Sunbird France 02 SAS moved the Delhi High Court to enforce a UK judgment directing SpiceJet to pay nearly $8 million in unpaid lease rentals and maintenance dues for three aircraft engines. SpiceJet questioned the petition's maintainability, citing issues with power of attorney and stamp duty. The court has directed the cash-strapped airline to file an affidavit disclosing its assets, adding to its mounting financial and litigation burdens.

articles.headline: TRAI Reviews Airtel's New 5G Slicing Priority Service
articles.summary: Bharti Airtel is migrating its 30-million postpaid customers to new 'Priority Postpaid' plans, leveraging 5G slicing technology for a superior network experience with consistent performance. TRAI is reviewing the service for net neutrality compliance, ensuring it doesn't degrade quality for prepaid users. Airtel aims to encourage prepaid-to-postpaid migration, a move backed by global network providers Nokia and Ericsson.

articles.headline: Adani Power to Acquire 24% JPVL Stake for Rs 4,194 Crore
articles.summary: Adani Power will acquire a 24% stake in Jaiprakash Power Ventures Ltd (JPVL) and the 180 MW Churk thermal power plant from Jaypee Group for approximately Rs 4,194 crore. This acquisition is part of a broader debt restructuring for the Jaypee Group and the implementation of an approved resolution plan for thermal assets, deepening Adani's presence in India's thermal power sector.

articles.headline: LIC Reports 23% Rise in Q4 Net Profit, Declares ₹10 Dividend
articles.summary: Life Insurance Corporation of India (LIC) reported a 23% rise in Q4 net profit to ₹23,467 crore, driven by premium growth and investment income, with total income up over 14% to ₹2.76 lakh crore. For the full fiscal, profit hit ₹57,453 crore. The board declared a final dividend of ₹10 per share, while CEO R. Doraiswamy cited record performance, cautioning geopolitical tensions could impact savings and economic activity.

articles.headline: India's Private Sector Activity Eases in May Amid Inflation
articles.summary: India's private sector activity eased marginally in May, as the HSBC Flash India Composite PMI slipped to 58.1 from 58.2. While services growth remained high, manufacturing output and new export orders slowed, dampening overall expansion. Input cost inflation, driven by energy and raw materials (steel, rubber), hit a near four-year high, reflecting impacts from the West Asia conflict, though the index remains above the 50-mark for growth.

articles.headline: India's Peak Power Demand Hits Record 270.82 GW Amid Heatwave
articles.summary: India's peak power demand hit a record 270.82 GW on Thursday, marking the fourth consecutive daily record in May, driven by intense heatwaves. This figure surpasses last summer's high of 243 GW. Thermal power contributed 62.8% and solar 22%. The power ministry expects demand to rise to 283 GW in June, with El Nino conditions in Northwest India potentially pushing it past 275 GW in coming weeks.

articles.headline: India Challenges UK Steel Safeguard Measures at WTO
articles.summary: India, joined by six other nations, raised concerns at the WTO over the UK's plan to limit tariff-free steel imports starting July 2026. The UK's new measures will reduce import quotas by 60%, imposing a 50% tariff on excess shipments. India requested less trade-restrictive solutions, as this issue has become a sticking point in implementing the India-UK comprehensive economic and trade agreement, signed in 2025.

articles.headline: Nykaa, Mamaearth Parents Report Strong Q4 Profit Growth
articles.summary: Nykaa parent FSN E-Commerce Ventures reported a Q4 net profit of ₹79 crore, up 300% from ₹19 crore, with revenue rising 28% to ₹2,648 crore and annual sales topping ₹10,000 crore. Growth was driven by omnichannel beauty/fashion, despite minor impacts from West Asia conflict on GCC operations. Separately, Mamaearth parent Honasa Consumer posted a Q4 net profit of ₹69.4 crore (up 178%), with revenue up 23% to ₹657 crore, and declared its first dividend.

articles.headline: Videocon Founder Dhoot Appeals to SC Over Foreign Assets Inclusion
articles.summary: Former Videocon chairman Venugopal Dhoot has appealed to the Supreme Court to include the group's overseas oil and gas assets, including holdings in Brazil and Indonesia, in the insolvency resolution process. Dhoot challenges an NCLAT ruling that excluded these assets, arguing they are critical to the resolution's value and that overseas units and domestic entities should be treated as a single economic entity for a smoother process.

articles.headline: SEBI Proposes New Price Discovery Rules for IPOs, Relistings
articles.summary: SEBI has proposed a new price discovery mechanism for IPOs and re-listings, aiming to prevent artificially suppressed prices and address persistent upper circuits on listing days. The changes to the pre-open call auction process include more dynamic widening of price bands (e.g., 10% automatically), new base price calculations based on traded prices or independent valuations, and requiring orders from at least five unique PAN-based investors for successful auctions.

articles.headline: Fino Payments Bank CEO Rishi Gupta Resigns for Early Retirement
articles.summary: Rishi Gupta resigned as MD and CEO of Fino Payments Bank for early retirement, shortly after the board approved his second term. CFO Ketan Merchant has been appointed interim CEO for three months, pending RBI approval. The leadership change comes as the bank, which previously saw Gupta involved in a GST legal probe, holds in-principle RBI approval to transition into a small finance bank.

articles.headline: Rajasthan Fuel Dealers Sue OMCs Over Alleged Sales Restrictions
articles.summary: Petrol pump dealers in Rajasthan, through their association, have moved the High Court against state-run oil marketing companies like IOC and BPCL. They allege OMCs are restricting fuel sales via informal messages and threats of punitive actions for exceeding limits. This dispute arises from surging demand at PSU stations due to lower prices compared to private retailers, leading to localized fuel shortages.

articles.headline: Iran Forbids Uranium Export, Hardening Stance in US Talks
articles.summary: Iran's Supreme Leader, Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei, issued a directive prohibiting the export of Iran’s near-weapons-grade uranium stockpile. This move hardens Tehran's stance in peace talks with the US and Israel, directly complicating US President Donald Trump’s demand for its removal. Iranian officials believe sending the enriched uranium abroad would leave the country vulnerable to future attacks by the US and Israel.

articles.headline: India Abstains from UN Climate Change Resolution
articles.summary: India abstained from a UN General Assembly resolution calling for countries to comply with climate change obligations. India expressed concern that the draft undermines the sacrosanct architecture of the UN Framework Convention on Climate Change and noted its clarification requests were not addressed during negotiations. The resolution was adopted with 141 votes in favor, eight against, and 28 abstentions.

articles.headline: Government Expands Youth Sports Development, Infrastructure Nationwide
articles.summary: Government initiatives have expanded youth sports development, focusing on grassroots talent identification and training nationwide. New sports infrastructure projects, including stadiums, training academies, and community sports complexes, are being developed across various regions to nurture future athletes, promote physical fitness among school-aged children, and enhance overall athletic talent.

articles.headline: Government Introduces New Regulations for Digital Media, Social Platforms
articles.summary: The government is introducing new regulations for digital media platforms and social media companies to ensure accountability, ethical content dissemination, and user privacy. These rules aim to balance freedom of expression with preventing misinformation and harmful online content, ultimately creating a safer online environment and ensuring compliance with national laws.
